content:
I graduated from Kyung Hee University in February 2022 with a BS in Computer Engineering. Two threads have defined my career so far: NLP research and AI engineering.
NLP Research My research has centered on language modeling with a bias toward problems that show up in real products. Two directions I have pursued: (1) Making language models more efficient — via knowledge distillation and augmented-memory architectures such as Product Key Memory. (2) Programming-language NLP — models that help developers write code, docstrings, and commit messages. I believe research is strongest when it is anchored to a shipping product, and that the ability to move work from R&D into production is a core skill for AI researchers.
AI Engineering As models get larger, the engineering needed to train them at scale — managing many GPU machines without CPU/GPU bottlenecks, running mixed precision correctly, and moving terabytes of training data efficiently — has become inseparable from the research itself. I have hands-on experience operating multi-node GPU clusters, designing efficient data pipelines (see my open-source project matorage), and building cloud infrastructure to serve trained models.
summary:
I graduated from Kyung Hee University in February 2022 with a BS in Computer Engineering. Two threads have defined my career so far: NLP research and AI engineering.
NLP Research My research has centered on language modeling with a bias toward problems that show up in real products.
title: Tae Hwan Jung

summary: Clober DEX core Solidity contracts — the on-chain central limit order book engine.
title: clober-dex/core

summary: Natural Language Processing Tutorial for Deep Learning Researchers
title: nlp-tutorial

summary: Shared Solidity library used across the Clober protocol stack, including gas-optimized data structures such as segmented segment trees and octopus heaps.
title: clober-dex/library

summary: A mind map and keyword roadmap for students interested in learning NLP.
title: nlp-roadmap

summary: TypeScript SDK for Clober DEX v2 — the integration surface for external builders and the company's own frontend.
title: clober-dex/v2-sdk

summary: A tutorial on the basic probability distributions used in deep learning research.
title: distribution-is-all-you-need

summary: The Graph subgraph that indexes Clober DEX v2 on-chain events for analytics, historical queries, and the SDK's read path.
title: clober-dex/v2-subgraph

summary: A VS Code extension that automatically generates Python docstrings using an NLP model.
title: ai-docstring

summary: Frontend for Coupon Finance — a fixed-rate DeFi lending protocol that tokenizes interest rates as tradable "coupons" on an on-chain order book.
title: clober-dex/coupon.finance

summary: A tool that AI automatically recommends commit messages.
title: commit-autosuggestions

summary: An object storage manager for tensors (multidimensional matrices), built for deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow V2, Keras).
title: matorage

summary: A simple PyTorch implementation of OpenAI's GPT-2 for text generation.
title: gpt-2-Pytorch

summary: A simple XLNet implementation with a PyTorch wrapper.
title: xlnet-Pytorch

summary: Introduces the model and data that generate a commit message when code diff is given using the pre-trained programming language model about six programming languages (Python, PHP, Go, Java, JavaScript, and Ruby). - [ACL NLP4Prog Workshop 2021](https://nlp4prog.github.io/2021/)
title: CommitBERT: Commit Message Generation Using Pre-Trained Programming Language Model

summary: Improving accuracy and speed trade-off when finetuning pretrained language models by using large product key memory and mitigating a catastrophic drift with initialization and residual memory. *(I was a research internship at Clova AI while doing this work.)* - [Findings of EMNLP 2020](https://2020.emnlp.org/)
title: Large Product Key Memory for Pretrained Language Models

summary: Federated Learning Infra Architecture on Kubernetes(EKS)
title: kubeFL

summary: A PyTorch implementation of ALBERT (A Lite BERT for Self-Supervised Learning of Language Representations).
title: ALBERT-Pytorch

summary: Undergraduate class project — solving TOEIC (Test of English for International Communication) questions using pytorch-pretrained-BERT.
title: toeicbert

summary: Model summaries for PyTorch, similar to `model.summary()` in Keras.
title: modelsummary
